name: Leuven

overview.description: Leuven is home to KU Leuven, founded in 1425 and one of the oldest universities in Europe, giving the city one of the highest concentrations of international students in Belgium — including a large Indian student community.
overview.foundedNote: KU Leuven founded in 1425, making it one of the oldest universities in Europe
whyVisit: KU Leuven University Library — rebuilt in neo-Renaissance style after WWI destruction, with a bell tower offering panoramic views
Oude Markt — a lively square lined with bars, popularly nicknamed 'the longest bar in Europe'
The Kruidtuin (Botanical Garden) — Belgium's oldest botanical garden, created in 1783 for medical students, free entry
An easy ~25 minute train ride from Brussels, making it a popular day trip or student base

attractions.name: KU Leuven University Library
attractions.description: Leuven's landmark building, with a famous Art Deco reading hall (weekends only) and a bell tower reachable via ~289 steps for panoramic city views.

attractions.name: Oude Markt
attractions.description: A historic square packed with bars and cafés, popularly known as 'Europe's longest bar'.

attractions.name: Kruidtuin (Botanical Garden)
attractions.description: Belgium's oldest botanical garden, established in 1783 by KU Leuven for medical students; free entry, around 800 plant species.

transportation.description: Direct trains to Brussels take roughly 25 minutes, making Leuven an easy commute or day trip.
The historic centre is compact and walkable; cycling is also very common among students.

hotels.name: City Centre (near Oude Markt / Grote Markt)
hotels.notes: Central and walkable to the university library and main square; can be lively/noisy near Oude Markt on weekends.

foodSpots.name: Restaurants and cafés around the student district
foodSpots.notes: A large international and Indian student population supports a growing range of South Asian grocery and food options near the university area.

tips: Leuven has one of the highest concentrations of international students in Belgium, including many Indian postgraduate students at KU Leuven.
The University Library's wooden Reading Hall is only open to visitors on weekends.
Being so close to Brussels (~25 min by train), Leuven works well as a lower-cost base for exploring the region.
